title: OpenClaw background worker OOM-killed silently with no restart policy

source.license_note: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #60388.

task.summary: Diagnose why the background job queue is silently draining with no workers processing tasks after a period of high load.
task.instructions: Use the incident evidence to confirm the worker was OOM-killed, localize the fault to the missing restart policy and absent memory limit, and submit a repair conclusion through the ops workflow.
task.acceptance_notes: This is an ops-only synthetic incident derived from a real GitHub issue.

visible_context.issue_text: After processing a large batch job, the background worker container stops processing new tasks. The queue depth grows but no errors are surfaced. The container is in Exited state with exit code 137.
error_logs: docker ps -a => worker container status=Exited (137) 14 minutes ago
kernel: Out of memory: Kill process 8821 (node) score 941
queue-monitor: depth=2048, consumers=0 — no active workers
reproduction_steps: Inspect the container exit code and kernel OOM logs.
Confirm no restart policy is configured and the memory limit is unset.
Decide whether the fault has been detected, localized, and repaired.
visible_context.notes: The expected repair sets a restart policy (e.g. unless-stopped) and adds a memory limit to prevent unbounded growth.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic incident for a background worker silently exiting after OOM kill with no configured restart policy.

constraints.notes: Ops-only smoke scenario. The standalone executor remains synthetic.

notes: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #60388.
